methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $7.5B - $78.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Scale AI, a Series-stage private company in Defense & Security, is currently valued at $29B following $1.6B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises ten comparable companies: two public entities and eight private firms operating in the same sector at similar valuation tiers, including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

The peer-median method applies here as the primary triangulation approach. Using a three-company comparable set filtered by sector and valuation tier—Anduril Industries ($78.0B, SPAC), Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), and Flock Safety ($7.5B, Series E)—the median valuation across this peer group is $9.2B. This method is constrained by wide dispersion within the sample; the range spans $7.5B to $78.0B, with Anduril representing a clear outlier following its SPAC transaction.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $7.9B to $10.6B, with a median point of $9.2B derived from peer multiples. The current $29B valuation substantially exceeds the comparable-company range, suggesting either that private market pricing reflects asymmetric information unavailable to this analysis, or that Scale AI commands a material premium relative to publicly-traded and late-stage private peers in the sector. Key uncertainties include the limited applicability of the Anduril SPAC transaction as a pricing benchmark and the early-stage status of several peer companies, which may understate appropriate valuations for a more mature, revenue-generating target.
